methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Atomic Semi is a Seed-stage semiconductor design company founded by Sam Zeloof and Jim Keller, having raised $15M+ to date. The Chips & Semiconductors sector contains 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist for reliable median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($15M) as a proxy for post-money value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to Series A produces an implied valuation of $38M. Public semiconductor comparables trade at a median 23.4x price-to-sales, but this anchor cannot be applied without the target's disclosed revenue, which is not available for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $43M (median $38M), derived entirely from stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties center on the absence of revenue data, scarcity of direct peer transactions at the Seed stage in semiconductors, and the wide dispersion in exit multiples across the peer set. This range should be treated as preliminary pending additional financial disclosure or comparable transaction evidence within the deep-tech hardware cohort.
